title: Openly-licensed pronunciation audio for language decks
context: Pronunciation is a critical component of language learning decks, but sourcing openly-licensed audio files is non-trivial. Existing pronunciation audio databases (e.g., Forvo, Wiktionary audio) have varying license terms that must be individually verified. This task is a sibling-project candidate with open-pronunciation-audio and should not duplicate that effort if it proceeds.
objective: Source, verify licenses for, and integrate openly-licensed pronunciation audio clips into language learning decks, with per-clip attribution and license verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Identifies openly-licensed audio sources (e.g., Wiktionary audio under CC-BY-SA, Forvo under CC-BY-NC or CC-BY where applicable) and verifies license terms per clip.
Produces a curated dataset of openly-licensed pronunciation audio clips with per-clip SPDX license identifier, source URL, attribution, and retrieval date.
Audio clips integrated into at least one language deck via the data-001 schema's media fields, with alt-text or transcript for accessibility.
All audio clips pass the license-verification gate (research-002 / code-003 rules applied to audio).
Dataset structured as a reusable collection in media/audio/ so other decks can reference the same files without duplication.
Dataset licensed under the most permissive license compatible with all included clip licenses.
output: A curated dataset of openly-licensed pronunciation audio clips (media/audio/) with attribution metadata and integration into at least one language deck CrowdAnki JSON file.
